primary_summary: Add an archive lifecycle to Mealie's ShoppingList entity: extend with archived_at + archived_by_user_id columns; new POST /archive and /unarchive endpoints; default GET filters archived lists out; ?archived=true|all reveals them; archived lists become immutable (PUT list / POST item / PUT item / DELETE item return 409 with i18n key shopping-list.archived.frozen); emit ShoppingListArchived/Unarchived events with payload restricted to owning household; multitenant isolation via existing HouseholdRepositoryGeneric scoping; centralised in mealie/repos/repository_shopping_list.py per input §7.

selected_approach.name: Soft delete + repository-level frozen guard
selected_approach.rationale: Selected per input §7 mandate 'centralised in repository_shopping_list.py'. Places both default-exclude filter AND frozen-state guard in the repository layer. Repos raise typed Python exception ShoppingListIsArchivedError (no HTTP/i18n concerns); service layer (or FastAPI global handler) catches and translates to HTTPException(409, ErrorResponse.respond(message=self.t('shopping-list.archived.frozen'))). Defense at persistence boundary: any future mutation route is auto-guarded. Existing RepositoryShoppingList.update override (lines 9-11) is the natural seam, expanded from passthrough into guarded passthrough.
candidates_considered: A. Soft delete (filter only, no guard) — rejected: violates input §3.
B. Soft delete + service-level guard — strong alternative but does not match input §7's 'centralised' wording as literally as Option C.
C. Soft delete + repository-level guard — SELECTED.

user_stories.title: Archive a shopping list (success path)
user_stories.i_want: to mark a fully-completed shopping list as archived
user_stories.so_that: the main shopping-list view stays uncluttered and I retain historical records
user_stories.acceptance: Given I am a member of household H1 and ShoppingList L1 has household_id == H1 and all items checked, when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/archive, then response is 200 OK with ShoppingListOut where archived_at is the current UTC timestamp and archived_by is my UserSummary; the row is persisted with archived_at IS NOT NULL and archived_by_user_id = me.id; one EventTypes.shopping_list_archived event is dispatched with EventShoppingListArchiveData payload.

user_stories.title: Archive blocked when unchecked items present
user_stories.i_want: the system to refuse to archive a list that still has unchecked items
user_stories.so_that: I don't accidentally lose track of in-progress purchases
user_stories.acceptance: Given ShoppingList L1 has any item with checked == None or checked == False, when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/archive, then response is 409 CONFLICT with detail.message == 'Cannot archive a shopping list while items remain unchecked' (en-US value of shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items); the row is unchanged; no event is dispatched.

user_stories.title: Default GET filters archived lists out
user_stories.i_want: the default shopping-lists view to show only active lists
user_stories.so_that: I see a clean current working set
user_stories.acceptance: Given household H1 contains L_active and L_archived, when I GET /api/households/shopping/lists, then response items contains L_active.id and not L_archived.id; total reflects active-only count.

user_stories.title: ?archived=true and ?archived=all reveal archive history
user_stories.i_want: explicit query parameters to view archived-only or all lists
user_stories.so_that: I can review history without polluting the default view
user_stories.acceptance: GET /api/households/shopping/lists?archived=true returns only archived lists with archived_at + archived_by populated; ?archived=all returns both active (archived_at: null, archived_by: null) and archived lists.

user_stories.title: Frozen-state: PUT list / POST item / PUT item / DELETE item return 409
user_stories.i_want: every mutating operation on an archived list (or its items) to be rejected with a clear frozen error
user_stories.so_that: archived history is preserved as a true historical record
user_stories.acceptance: Given ShoppingList L1 has archived_at IS NOT NULL, when I attempt PUT /lists/{L1.id} OR POST /items (targeting L1) OR POST /items/create-bulk OR PUT /items/{I.id} OR PUT /items OR DELETE /items/{I.id} OR DELETE /items (bulk), then each response is 409 CONFLICT with detail.message == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.' (en-US value of shopping-list.archived.frozen); no rows mutated; updated_at unchanged.

user_stories.title: Unarchive restores mutable state
user_stories.i_want: to unarchive a list back to mutable state
user_stories.so_that: I can correct mistakes or repurpose an archived list
user_stories.acceptance: Given ShoppingList L1 has archived_at IS NOT NULL, when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L1.id}/unarchive, then response is 200 OK with archived_at == null and archived_by == null; the row has archived_at IS NULL and archived_by_user_id IS NULL; L1 reappears in default GET /lists; subsequent PUT /lists and item mutations succeed; one EventTypes.shopping_list_unarchived event is dispatched.

user_stories.title: Event bus dispatches with correct payload (no cross-household leak)
user_stories.as_a: webhook/notifier subscriber configured for shopping-list-archive events in my household
user_stories.i_want: to receive exactly the spec'd payload fields on archive/unarchive
user_stories.so_that: I can integrate downstream systems without leaking other tenants' data
user_stories.acceptance: Subscribed via GroupEventNotifier with shopping_list_archived = true for H1; when any user in H1 archives a list, exactly one Event arrives with event_type == EventTypes.shopping_list_archived and document_data of type EventShoppingListArchiveData containing exactly fields {document_type, operation, shopping_list_id, shopping_list_name, household_id, archived_by_user_id, item_count, total_estimated_amount} and no additional fields; payload household_id equals H1; no Event arrives at subscribers in other households; symmetric for shopping_list_unarchived.

user_stories.title: Multitenant isolation
user_stories.as_a: member of household H1 in group G1
user_stories.i_want: archived shopping lists to remain invisible to other households and groups
user_stories.acceptance: Given H1 owns archived L1; H2 (same group G1) owns archived L2; H3 (group G2) owns archived L3; when I (in H1) GET /api/households/shopping/lists?archived=true, items contains only L1.id; when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L2.id}/archive OR /unarchive, response is 404 NOT FOUND; when I POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{L3.id}/archive, response is 404 NOT FOUND.

user_stories.title: Schema backward compatibility
user_stories.as_a: existing API consumer that does not read archived_at/archived_by
user_stories.i_want: the existing ShoppingListOut / ShoppingListSummary shapes to remain backward-compatible
user_stories.so_that: my pre-feature code continues to work without changes
user_stories.acceptance: Default GET response items have archived_at: null and archived_by: null which existing consumers ignore; all existing tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py (1113 lines, 7 baseline test methods) continue to pass unchanged.

user_stories.title: Scheduled cleanup respects frozen state
user_stories.so_that: archived history is preserved against automatic trimming
user_stories.acceptance: When the scheduled task runs against a household with both active and archived lists having >100 checked items each, active list is trimmed (existing behavior); archived list left fully intact; no 409 exceptions logged from scheduler context.

Cross-household / cross-group access returns 404 via existing HouseholdRepositoryGeneric._filter_builder (no extra code)
Return updated ShoppingListOut with archived_at + archived_by populated (archive) or null (unarchive)

POST /api/households/shopping/items (lines 127-129) returns 409 on archived parent

functional_requirements.title: Repository layer — centralised archive filter + guard (RepositoryShoppingList)
must_haves: update(item_id, data) override extends existing override at lines 9-11: fetch row via self.session.get(self.model, item_id); raise ShoppingListIsArchivedError({item_id}) if archived_at is not None; else super().update(...)

archive(item_id, user_id) -> ShoppingListOut: raw SQLAlchemy update(...).values(archived_at=datetime.now(UTC), archived_by_user_id=user_id) — bypasses guarded update; raises ShoppingListIsArchivedError if already archived (idempotent failure)
unarchive(item_id) -> ShoppingListOut: raw SQLAlchemy update(...).values(archived_at=None, archived_by_user_id=None); idempotent — unarchiving an active list is a no-op (NOT 409)
page_all override accepts archived: ArchivedFilter = ArchivedFilter.exclude; composes .where(ShoppingList.archived_at.is_(None | not None)) on top of _filter_builder
get_archived_ids(ids: set[UUID4]) -> set[UUID4]: bulk helper, single SQL query, respects existing tenant scoping

functional_requirements.title: Service layer — archive_list / unarchive_list + 409 translation
must_haves: ShoppingListService.archive_list(self, list_id: UUID4, user_id: UUID4) -> ShoppingListOut: fetch list, 404 if None; raise 409 if already archived (shopping-list.archived.frozen); validate all items checked (raise 409 with shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items if any item.checked is None or False); call self.shopping_lists.archive(list_id, user_id); return

Wrap bulk_create_items / bulk_update_items / bulk_delete_items bodies in try/except ShoppingListIsArchivedError -> HTTPException(409, ErrorResponse.respond(message=t('shopping-list.archived.frozen'))). Translator dependency injection deferred to coding phase (see Self-concern SC-1)
Do NOT wrap archive_list / unarchive_list bodies — those call repo methods directly, not bulk

success_criteria.title: Archive idempotency
success_criteria.measurement: Calling POST /archive on already-archived list returns 409 (not 200, not 500). Integration test asserts response.status_code == 409 and detail.message == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.'

success_criteria.title: All 4 frozen routes return 409 with i18n message
success_criteria.measurement: 7 distinct integration test methods covering each frozen route variant (PUT list, POST item, POST items/create-bulk, PUT item, PUT items, DELETE item, DELETE items). Each asserts status_code == 409 AND detail.message matches en-US value of shopping-list.archived.frozen

success_criteria.title: Event payload field-set is exactly the spec'd set
success_criteria.measurement: Test using monkeypatch.setattr on EventBusService.dispatch captures dispatched events; asserts set(payload.model_dump().keys()) == {'document_type', 'operation', 'shopping_list_id', 'shopping_list_name', 'household_id', 'archived_by_user_id', 'item_count', 'total_estimated_amount'} for shopping_list_archived and shopping_list_unarchived events

success_criteria.title: Event payload household isolation
success_criteria.measurement: Test where user U1 in household H1 archives list L1; captured dispatch shows household_id == H1.id (NOT H2.id, NOT None)

success_criteria.title: Default GET filter correctness
success_criteria.measurement: Three integration tests: (a) GET /lists returns only active list when one of each exists; total==1. (b) GET /lists?archived=true returns only archived list; total==1. (c) GET /lists?archived=all returns both; total==2

success_criteria.title: Multitenant isolation — same-group different-household
success_criteria.measurement: Using h2_user fixture: H2 user calls GET /lists?archived=true → response items does not contain any H1 list. H2 user calls POST /lists/{H1_list.id}/archive → response status is 404

success_criteria.title: Multitenant isolation — cross-group
success_criteria.measurement: ArchivedShoppingListsTestCase registered in tests/multitenant_tests/test_multitenant_cases.py:13-19 all_cases; parametrized test_multitenant_cases_get_all automatically asserts user_two (different group) sees empty list when user_one's group has archived lists seeded

success_criteria.title: i18n key presence
success_criteria.measurement: Unit test asserts Translator(locale='en-US').t('shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items') == 'Cannot archive a shopping list while items remain unchecked' AND t('shopping-list.archived.frozen') == 'This shopping list is archived and cannot be modified. Unarchive it first.' (no KeyError)

success_criteria.title: Migration reversibility
success_criteria.measurement: alembic upgrade head then alembic downgrade -2 cleanly removes both archive columns and both notifier-options columns. Existing rows preserved. Verified on CI matrix for SQLite + PostgreSQL

success_criteria.title: Test count by category
success_criteria.measurement: ≥4 unit tests (archive_list success, archive_list 409 unchecked-items, unarchive_list success, RepositoryShoppingList.page_all ArchivedFilter branches); ≥10 integration tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py covering US-1..US-7 + US-10; ≥5 multitenant tests (1 new case_shopping_list_archive.py + ≥4 in test_shopping_list_archive_household.py)

success_criteria.title: Backward compatibility — existing tests pass
success_criteria.measurement: All currently-passing tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py (1113 lines, 7+ baseline test methods) and test_group_shopping_list_items.py (623 lines) continue to pass unmodified via task py:test

success_criteria.title: Scheduler does not regress
success_criteria.measurement: Unit test for delete_old_checked_list_items: given household with 1 archived list (150 checked items) and 1 active list (150 checked items), task runs; archived list still has 150 items, active has 100 (MAX_CHECKED_ITEMS); no HTTPException or ShoppingListIsArchivedError raised

edge_cases.title: Archive an empty list (no items)
edge_cases.expected: POST /archive succeeds (all-checked precondition vacuously true). L1.archived_at set. Event dispatched with item_count == 0
edge_cases.why_matters: all(item.checked for item in []) evaluates True (correct), but downstream consumers might assume non-empty payload

edge_cases.title: Archive when exactly 1 item is unchecked
edge_cases.scenario: L1 has 9 items with checked=True, 1 with checked=False
edge_cases.expected: POST /archive returns 409 with shopping-list.archive.unchecked-items message. List NOT archived
edge_cases.why_matters: Boundary case for the precondition logic; ensures 'any unchecked' check is short-circuiting correctly

edge_cases.title: Archive when 1 item has checked = None (NULL in DB)
edge_cases.scenario: L1 has 9 items with checked=True, 1 with checked=None
edge_cases.expected: POST /archive returns 409 — NULL treated as unchecked
edge_cases.why_matters: ShoppingListItem.checked: FilterableColumn[bool | None] (shopping_list.py:65) allows NULL; precondition must treat NULL as unchecked

edge_cases.title: Unarchive a non-archived list
edge_cases.expected: POST /unarchive returns 200 OK with list unchanged (idempotent no-op). NO event dispatched
edge_cases.why_matters: Asymmetric from archive-already-archived (which is 409). Intentional: archive failure signals re-archive attempt; unarchive-on-active is benign cleanup

edge_cases.title: Archive cascade — downstream consumers
edge_cases.scenario: Multiple downstream consumers of shopping lists
edge_cases.expected: Cookbook: no interaction (cookbooks are recipe collections, not list consumers). Meal plan: one-way flow into shopping list, no interaction back. delete_old_checked_shopping_list_items.py scheduler: WOULD trigger 409s in cron context; fix mandated in FR-14. Backup/export (backups_v2/): dumps full ORM rows, new columns flow automatically. Frontend offline PWA queue: receives 409 on flush after archive while items queued offline; recommended (out of v1) to drop failing op rather than retry
edge_cases.why_matters: Input §三环节考察点 explicitly asks 'have you enumerated all downstream consumers?' This is the inventory

edge_cases.title: Backup/export of archived lists
edge_cases.scenario: Operator runs POST /api/admin/backups after some lists archived
edge_cases.expected: Backup contains all shopping_lists rows including archived_at + archived_by_user_id columns. Restore re-creates with same archived state
edge_cases.why_matters: Verified via existing backup-roundtrip test inheriting new columns automatically; no new test required for v1

edge_cases.title: Admin force-unarchive
edge_cases.scenario: Per input §三环节考察点 — should admin be able to force-unarchive another household's list?
edge_cases.expected: NOT in v1 scope. Existing BaseAdminController (base_controllers.py:180-189) clears household scoping so admin sees ALL households' archived lists via /api/admin/... routes. No admin-specific archive endpoint added in v1. The regular archive_one/unarchive_one inherit BaseCrudController — admin succeeds only if belongs to owning household
edge_cases.why_matters: Input §三环节考察点 explicitly flags this. Tracked in self_concerns for v2

edge_cases.title: Concurrent archive race
edge_cases.scenario: U1 and U2 both in H1; both POST /archive on L1 simultaneously when L1 is active
edge_cases.expected: One succeeds (race winner sets archived_by_user_id). Other receives 409 + shopping-list.archived.frozen because by the time RepositoryShoppingList.archive runs, sees archived_at IS NOT NULL
edge_cases.why_matters: Without the already-archived check inside archive method, both writes commit and second silently overwrites archived_by_user_id. FR-7 §2 mandates the guard. Out of scope for v1: unique partial index WHERE archived_at IS NULL for stronger DB-level guarantee

edge_cases.title: User who archived a list is deleted
edge_cases.scenario: U1 archives L1; later U1's account is deleted
edge_cases.expected: FK declared with ondelete='SET NULL' (FR-2). User deletion succeeds. L1.archived_by_user_id becomes NULL. Tested via integration test: delete user who archived list, re-fetch list, assert archived_by_user_id is None
edge_cases.why_matters: Without ON DELETE SET NULL, user deletion would fail with FK constraint violation. archived_by_user_id is nullable attribution field — SET NULL is the most correct semantic

needs_clarification.question: Should PUT /api/households/shopping/lists/{id}/label-settings and the recipe-management routes also return 409 on archived lists?
needs_clarification.conflict: Spec §3 enumerates exactly 4 routes. Label-settings bypasses RepositoryShoppingList.update (uses shopping_list_multi_purpose_labels.update_many at line 243), so repo guard does NOT cover it. Recipe-add/remove go through guarded bulk methods AND call self.shopping_lists.update at line 454 — that call WILL trigger the guard producing a partially-applied-then-409 error path
needs_clarification.default_resolution: v1 freezes ONLY the 4 spec'd routes + their bulk siblings. Label-settings remains mutable on archived lists. Recipe-add/remove produce partial mutation failure path that is acceptable in v1; documented in PR description

needs_clarification.question: Scope of 'household 内成员' rule — admin-only or any household member?
needs_clarification.conflict: Input §2 says 'household 内成员' (household member) for archive/unarchive endpoints. Existing CRUD routes on ShoppingListController use default BaseCrudController permission (any authenticated user whose household_id matches), NOT BaseAdminController
needs_clarification.default_resolution: Any authenticated household member can archive/unarchive. No admin role required. Consistent with existing routes

needs_clarification.question: Exact event payload field names — list_id vs shopping_list_id?
needs_clarification.conflict: Spec §5 lists payload fields as list_id, list_name, household_id, archived_by_user_id, item_count, total_estimated_amount. Existing EventShoppingListData uses shopping_list_id. Should new payload use spec's exact list_id/list_name or existing convention?
needs_clarification.default_resolution: Use existing-code convention: shopping_list_id and shopping_list_name. Rationale: (a) consistency with EventShoppingListData.shopping_list_id and EventShoppingListItemBulkData.shopping_list_id; (b) spec's list_id is shorthand that creates ambiguity (meal_plan_id, cookbook_id collisions); (c) any auto-generated TypeScript consumer prefers long form. Spec's list_id interpreted as informal shorthand for shopping_list_id

needs_clarification.question: total_estimated_amount semantics — no price column exists on ShoppingListItem today
needs_clarification.conflict: Spec §5 says payload includes total_estimated_amount (如有 / if available). No price / cost / amount column on ShoppingListItem (verified)
needs_clarification.default_resolution: Field defaults to None. Forward-compat hook for future price-tracking feature. NOT computed from any existing column. Coding phase MUST NOT attempt computation in v1

needs_clarification.question: ON DELETE behavior for archived_by_user_id FK
needs_clarification.conflict: When user who archived a list is deleted, what happens to archived_by_user_id?
needs_clarification.default_resolution: ON DELETE SET NULL. Migration FR-2 declares batch_op.create_foreign_key(..., ondelete='SET NULL'). See EC-8

self_concerns.detail: FR-11 requires ShoppingListService.bulk_* methods to call self.t(...) to translate ShoppingListIsArchivedError. Service constructor today (shopping_lists.py:37-43) takes ONLY repos: AllRepositories — no translator. Adding translator: Translator to __init__ is a breaking change for every caller (controller, scheduler, test fixtures)
self_concerns.recommended_resolution: Register a global exception handler in mealie/routes/handlers.py that maps ShoppingListIsArchivedError -> HTTPException(409, ErrorResponse.respond(...)). The handler accesses request's translator via FastAPI dependency machinery. Cleanest: avoids ShoppingListService signature change AND keeps i18n at the HTTP boundary

self_concerns.concern: Partial-mutation failure path for recipe-add/recipe-remove routes
self_concerns.detail: Recipe-add/remove routes call BOTH guarded bulk_create_items AND the guarded update on the list (line 454: self.shopping_lists.update(updated_list.id, updated_list)). bulk_create_items could succeed creating items; subsequent update could fail with 409 if list is archived. User sees 409 but DB has new items
self_concerns.recommended_resolution: Acceptable in v1 since these routes aren't in spec §3's frozen list. Document partial-failure mode in PR description. v2 could wrap the entire operation in a transaction with savepoint rollback if user complaints surface

self_concerns.concern: RepositoryShoppingListItem constructor — passing the parent repo
self_concerns.detail: RepositoryShoppingListItem needs self.repos.group_shopping_lists.get_archived_ids(...) but HouseholdRepositoryGeneric.__init__ doesn't take AllRepositories reference (repository_generic.py:505-523)
self_concerns.recommended_resolution: Pass parent_repo: RepositoryShoppingList to constructor; extends factory wiring at repository_factory.py:325. Cleanest single source of truth, explicit dependency. Adds ~3 lines to factory

self_concerns.detail: Adding archived_by: UserSummary | None to ShoppingListSummary.loader_options adds one more selectinload per list. For households with hundreds of lists, this is small N+1 risk. Existing loader_options at line 224-238 already eager-loads User via joinedload(ShoppingList.user); new archived_by eager-load is separate join (different FK)
self_concerns.recommended_resolution: Acceptable in v1. Only loaded eagerly when needed. Since archived_by defaults to None and is populated only when archived, the join effectively becomes LEFT OUTER JOIN that is nearly free for active-list-heavy responses. Performance test not required for v1; benchmark in v2 if reports surface

new_files: mealie/core/exceptions.py (add ShoppingListIsArchivedError, or co-locate in repository_shopping_list.py)
modified_files: mealie/routes/handlers.py (if SCN-1 resolution is to register global exception handler)

compliance_checklist: All Python commands use uv run … (not raw python/pip)
Run task py:check (Ruff + mypy + pytest) before commit
Run task dev:generate after any Pydantic schema or EventTypes change; commit regenerated files
All new SQLAlchemy columns use FilterableColumn[…] wrapper per GHSA-8m57-7cv5-rjp8
Migrations use op.batch_alter_table(...) for SQLite + PostgreSQL portability
Only mealie/lang/messages/en-US.json touched among locale files; all others are Crowdin-managed
Repository methods inherit/call _filter_builder to preserve multitenancy scoping
Service methods stay free of HTTP concerns; HTTP translation lives at controller or exception-handler layer
No hand-edits to frontend/app/lib/api/types/, mealie/schema/*/__init__.py, or tests/utils/api_routes/__init__.py — all autogen
PR title follows Conventional Commits (e.g. 'feat: add archive lifecycle to shopping lists')
PR description includes release notes and ADR-style rationale for repo-layer guard placement
